changes.oldText: To ensure separation of duties, management of the audit logs shall be a different individual other than the SA. A privileged group shall be established to access audit information and support systems.
changes.newText: Replaced by: 'Audit records must be stored at least weekly in a repository that is part of a physically different system or system component than the system or component being audited. Cryptographic mechanisms must be implemented to protect the integrity of audit information and audit tools.'
changes.rationale: PROCEDURE IMPACT — HIGH: logging architecture must ship audit records to a physically separate repository at least weekly (e.g., SIEM/log server) and add cryptographic integrity protection (signing/hashing). The people-based separation-of-duties wording is gone in favor of technical controls.

changes.title: AU-2 Event Logging — call-recording search capability consolidated here; 'customer' → 'taxpayer'
changes.oldText: Call recording application systems must have the ability to capture and retain call recording meta-data. Audit records shall be sufficient to enable re-creation of information system related events. (Search capability was stated under AU-6.)
changes.newText: Contractors shall configure call recording systems to capture and retain meta-data 'and have the capability to search the meta-data for the purposes of play-back, quality assurance, and a record of file access.' Meta-data must track the identity of the 'taxpayer' (was 'customer'). The re-creation sentence was removed.
changes.rationale: Consolidation plus keyword changes; the search requirement moved from AU-6 into AU-2.

changes.title: CA-5 POA&M — trigger changed from CSA final report to CSA initial report
changes.oldText: When a CSA final report is issued to the COR, the contractor shall create and provide an initial POA&M.
changes.newText: When a CSA initial report is issued to the COR, the contractor must create and provide an initial POA&M within 30 business days.
changes.rationale: Keyword change with schedule effect: POA&M development now starts at the initial assessment report rather than the final one — earlier in the assessment cycle.

changes.title: CA-6 Authorization — authorization package contents revised
changes.oldText: Final authorization packages: System Security Plan (SSP), Privacy Program Plan, Security Assessment Report (SAR), PCLIA, Active POA&Ms, Memorandum of Understanding (MOU), Auditing Plan, Security Authorization Decision Document. The SSP shall contain (internally or as appendices): SRA, Interconnection Agreement, Contingency Plan, Security Configuration Documentation, CM Plan, IR Plan, Continuous Monitoring Plan, Implemented Security Controls.
changes.newText: Final authorization packages: Security and Privacy Plans, Security and Privacy Assessment Reports, PCLIA, Active POA&Ms, Executive Summary, Auditing Plan, and Authorization Decision Document. The MOU deliverable and the itemized list of required SSP appendices were removed.
changes.rationale: PROCEDURE IMPACT — MEDIUM: authorization package checklists must be revised — MOU out, Executive Summary in, and the prescriptive SSP-appendix list no longer applies.

changes.title: CA-8 Penetration Testing — independent team required; production-entry trigger added
changes.oldText: The contractor shall conduct external penetration testing on information systems at a minimum every three years. An independent penetration agent or team shall be employed to conduct penetration tests of the CSP information system.
changes.newText: The Contractor must conduct penetration testing 'using an independent team when an information system is put into production and at a minimum every three years thereafter.' CSP systems: independent penetration agent or team at least annually.
changes.rationale: PROCEDURE IMPACT — HIGH: the independence requirement now applies to contractor systems (not only CSPs), and go-live of a new system triggers a penetration test.

changes.title: SC-13 Cryptographic Protection — NEW TLS 1.2/1.3 mandate per NIST SP 800-52 Rev 2
changes.oldText: IRS SBU data processed, stored, or transmitted shall be protected using FIPS 140-2 or later validated encryption. (No TLS version requirement stated in this control.)
changes.newText: Adds: 'Contractor and CSP information systems must be configured to use TLS 1.2 and support TLS 1.3; if interoperability permits, TLS 1.3 is preferred. TLS must be implemented in accordance with NIST SP 800-52 Rev 2.' SC-23 Session Authenticity similarly now requires 'implementing TLS 1.2 or later'.
changes.rationale: PROCEDURE IMPACT — HIGH: system baselines must disable pre-1.2 TLS, enable 1.3 where possible, and align cipher configuration with SP 800-52r2.

changes.title: SC-28 Protection of Information at Rest — encryption scope 'portable media' → 'all digital media'
changes.oldText: All portable media shall be encrypted, including laptops, USB storage devices, backup media.
changes.newText: All digital media must employ FIPS 140-2 or later validated encryption modules, including laptops, USB storage devices, backup media.
changes.rationale: Keyword/scope change: at-rest encryption with validated modules now covers all digital media, not only portable media.

changes.title: SA-9 External System Services — providers must employ 'IRS Publication 4812 baseline' controls
changes.oldText: Require providers of external information system services to comply with information security and privacy requirements and employ adequate security and privacy controls.
changes.newText: Require providers of external information system services to comply with IRS security and privacy requirements and employ 'IRS Publication 4812 baseline security and privacy controls.'
changes.rationale: Keyword tightening: vendor flow-down must now reference the 4812 baseline explicitly rather than a generic adequacy standard.

changes.title: SA-11 Developer Testing — static/dynamic testing required throughout the life cycle
changes.oldText: Perform system testing and evaluation that includes one or more of: design, implementation representation (source code/hardware schematics). Produce evidence of the execution of the security and privacy assessment plan and the results of the security testing and evaluation.
changes.newText: Perform system testing and evaluation '(including static and/or dynamic) throughout its life cycle'; document the results of the security testing/evaluation 'and flaw remediation processes'. (The verifiable flaw-remediation-process bullet already existed in 2024 and is unchanged.)
changes.rationale: Requirement addition for development done for the IRS: static and/or dynamic testing (SAST/DAST) is now explicitly required across the SDLC, not just at a single evaluation point.

changes.title: SI-3 Malicious Code Protection — VDI added to coverage; explicit daily-update wording replaced
changes.oldText: Virus protection software shall be installed on all workstations, servers, and mobile computing devices, and shall be configured to perform automated updates daily. Contractors provisioned with GFEs shall not access, view, or download personal email.
changes.newText: Malicious code protection software must be installed on all workstations, servers, 'virtual desktop infrastructure (VDI)', and mobile computing devices, and must include 'automated signature and engine updates' with centralized management (the explicit 'daily' update frequency no longer appears). The GFE personal-email prohibition sentence was removed from this control (broader GFE email restrictions remain).
changes.rationale: Keyword changes: VDI joins the mandatory-coverage list; update cadence is now 'automated' without a stated daily minimum.

changes.title: SR-2 Supply Chain Risk Management Plan — SCRM team required
changes.oldText: Contractors shall establish and implement a SCRM plan and review/update it annually.
changes.newText: Adds: 'A supply chain risk management team must be established to lead and support SCRM activities.' SR-3 likewise adds: 'The Contractor must document the selected and implemented supply chain processes and controls in the supply chain risk management plan.'
changes.rationale: PROCEDURE IMPACT — MEDIUM: SCRM can no longer be a document-only exercise — a named team with a lead-and-support role is required, and control selections must be documented in the plan.

changes.title: PT-7 Social Security Numbers — Form 14132 applicability check added
changes.oldText: Contractors shall take steps to eliminate unnecessary uses of SSNs.
changes.newText: Contractors must take steps to eliminate unnecessary uses of SSNs 'and contact privacy about the applicability of Form 14132.'
changes.rationale: New procedural step referencing the IRS SSN-use justification form.

changes.title: Section 33.0 Privacy — three-page IRM 10.5.1 section listing removed
changes.oldText: Section 33.0 enumerated roughly 120 individual IRM 10.5.1 subsections (10.5.1.1 through 10.5.1-1 Glossary) that apply to contractors.
changes.newText: Section 33.0 keeps the IRM 10.5.1 applicability statement and link (irs.gov/irm/part10/irm_10-005-001) but drops the itemized subsection list.
changes.rationale: Editorial condensation — the obligation ('publicly available sections of IRM 10.5.1 apply to the contractor') is unchanged.
